title: Fast Local Feedback, Hooks, and Developer Experience · Broken-Build Bay
summary: Create fast local gates that match CI without blocking ordinary iteration. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a one-command local verification workflow and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Expose stable format, lint, type, test, and verify commands independent of tool internals.
Run fast deterministic changed-file checks in local hooks and keep heavier checks elsewhere.
Treat local hooks as convenience while CI remains authoritative and bypasses stay visible.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

content.text: For Command Interface, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a gates-feedback-quality-tzeadl: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Feedback Quality into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-feedback-quality-tzeadl:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Feedback Quality applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Make failures concise, reproducible locally, and linked to remediation.
